channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: El Gobierno relanza el Sandbox financiero para darle más agilidad y seguridad
item.description: El Gobierno trabaja en una reforma del Sandbox financiero con el objetivo de agilizar y facilitar la experimentación de proyectos innovadores. La medida se incluye en el Anteproyecto de Ley de Digitalización y Modernización del Sector Financiero, que será remitido esta semana al Congreso.

item.title: El 72% de los mediadores detecta más demanda de ciberseguros, pero un tercio no sabe explicarlos
item.description: El crecimiento del interés por los ciberseguros no está yendo acompañado de un aumento equivalente en la capacidad de los mediadores para comercializarlos.

item.title: RibéSalat cierra 2025 con un crecimiento del 24,5% en primas intermediadas
item.description: RibéSalat alcanzó el pasado ejercicio un volumen de primas intermediadas de 1.370 millones de euros, lo que supone un crecimiento del 24,5%.

item.title: Workers at Kentucky Candle Plant Not Limited to Comp Claims, Appellate Court Finds
item.description: Employees who were killed and injured when a powerful tornado struck a Kentucky candle factory in 2021 are not necessarily limited to the workers’ compensation exclusive remedy, a Kentucky appeals court said in a decision that could have far-reaching impact for employers and commercial liability insurers. The Kentucky workers’ compensation statute, like most states’ laws, allows an exception to the exclusive remedy and grand bargain of the comp system if an injury or death is caused by an employer’s deliberate, aggressive actions, the court noted. The Kentucky Court of Appeals found that the workers can sue Mayfield Consumer Products and a supervisor for damages because the employer had threatened the workers with termination and had unlawfully intimidated or restrained them from leaving the building before the EF-4 twister struck.

item.title: US Hits Iran With Strikes, Blockade as Trump Plans Hormuz Charge
item.description: New York time, the US had begun launching a “third consecutive night of strikes against Iran.” Trump had vowed the US would keep up attacks on Iran, saying in a radio interview that “we’re going to hit them very hard tonight, and we’re going to hit them hard tomorrow.” Later in the Oval Office, he told reporters the strikes were aimed at targeting Iran’s capabilities “for anything having to do with the strait.” Brent crude rose above $85 a barrel for the first time in a month after Trump reimposed the blockade and demanded payment for cargo moving through the strait. President Donald Trump reinstated the US blockade of Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and demanded a 20% reimbursement for all other cargo shipped through the waterway. A reinstatement of the blockade on Iranian ports may prompt the Islamic Republic to step up attacks on ships seeking to transit the Strait of Hormuz, which would continue a pattern of tit-for-tat strikes over the past week.

item.title: Clash of Florida Titans Pits Powerful Tribe Against Homebuilder Lennar
item.description: But the Seminole tribe’s sovereign status has so far kept their fight in the courts, resulting in a highly visible clash between two powerful players in Florida’s real estate- and tourism-focused economy. The Seminole Tribe of Florida hired Miami-based home construction giant Lennar Corp. “We wanted to keep our community together.” Last year, the Seminoles sued Lennar, claiming defects in more than 500 homes built on six different reservations across Florida, including the one near Hollywood.

item.title: Sompo taps Canon Liu as head of financial lines for Hong Kong, Macau and Taiwan
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: APAC transaction liability transforms into ‘mature and established’ risk class: Aon
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: January Victoria bushfire losses rise to US$596m: Perils
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Ategrity expects $205m GWP with CoR under 87% for Q2’26
item.description: Ategrity expects to report record-breaking numbers across all core financial metrics for Q2 2026, driven by broad-based growth in both property and casualty lines. Additionally, Ategrity expects to see record diluted earnings per share of more than $0.60 in Q2 2026. Ategrity Specialty Insurance Company Holdings has announced its preliminary financial results for the second quarter of 2026, reporting gross written premiums (GWP) of more than $205 million and a combined ratio below 87%.

item.title: Rokstone adds Claire Lill to North American Casualty team
item.description: Rokstone, the international specialty re/insurance managing general agent (MGA), has announced the addition of Claire Lill to its North American Casualty team. Lill joins Rokstone’s established North American Casualty leadership team, which includes Larry Burrows, Ben Baker, Alex Fitzpatrick, and Lex Beesley. Larry Burrows, Head of North American Casualty at Rokstone, said, “Claire has one of the strongest reputations in the market and brings a great deal of discipline, technical expertise, and strong broker relationships to the wider team.

item.title: Jensten Group expands Scottish presence with Kelvin Smith Insurance acquisition
item.description: Jensten Group, a UK insurance broking and financial services group, has agreed to acquire Glasgow-based broker Kelvin Smith Insurance, subject to regulatory approval. Stephen Travers, Managing Director of Kelvin Smith Insurance, commented: “Joining Jensten is a fantastic opportunity for us to grow our business further within Scotland. Scotland is a key strategic priority for Jensten, and with Broker One, NC Stirling and now Kelvin Smith Insurance, we are bringing together outstanding teams who share our client-first approach and growth mindset.”

item.title: Florida Property Tax Cut Plan Relies on Population Boom That Has Slowed Dramatically
item.description: From 2020 to 2024, Florida’s population grew by 8.5%, from 21.6 million to 23.4 million. This is nothing new: The state’s warm weather, amenities that include world-class golf courses and beaches, and lack of income tax have long attracted newcomers, so Florida often leads the U.S. But recent data suggests that population growth may be slowing .

item.title: FDA Urges Infant-Formula Makers to Tighten Supplier Oversight After Product Recalls
item.description: FDA on July 13 urged the infant-formula industry and supply-chain partners to exercise substantive oversight of their suppliers following multiple product recalls and recent botulism cases – the first outbreaks in the country linked to baby formula in nearly 50 years. The Food and Drug Administration has so far investigated two multi-state outbreaks of infant botulism associated with two separate brands of powdered infant formula – ByHeart and Nara Organics – that resulted in the products being recalled , it said in its letter to manufacturers, packagers, distributors, exporters, importers, and retailers of infant formula. botulism outbreak linked to infant formula in nearly 50 years , according to the agency.

item.title: People Moves: The Baldwin Group Hires Pierce as West Regional President for Insurance Advisory Solutions
item.description: The Baldwin Group announced the appointment of Phil Pierce as west regional president for its Insurance Advisory Solutions (IAS) segment. In this leadership role, Pierce will be responsible for the strategic direction, growth, and operational excellence of Baldwin’s IAS business across the West Region, including talent development and partner relationships. With 20 years of industry experience, Pierce is a proven leader with a strong track record for driving growth, leading high-performing teams, and advising organizations on complex risk management strategies.

item.title: Plymouth Rock Names Three Executives
item.description: (Image credit: IIR/Gemini.) Plymouth Rock Assurance (Boston) has appointed Gavin McPhail VP and Chief Data Officer; Cornelius Young VP and Chief Claims Officer, Independent Agency Group; and Lindsay Mustard VP, Customer Service, Independent Agency Group. Andrew McElwee, President and COO, The Plymouth Rock Company. The appointments are intended to support Plymouth Rock’s work in innovation, operational excellence and customer experience, according to a company statement.

item.title: CFC adds Gemma Hicks to Australian cyber team
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Carriers navigating emerging drone, eVTOL risks with measured caution as China’s low-altitude economy takes off
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: KKR joins US$5.4bn Steadfast bid
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Accelerant partners with WoodStar
item.description: Accelerant has partnered with newly formed reciprocal insurer WoodStar Reciprocal Exchange, which will provide dedicated underwriting capacity exclusively through the Accelerant Risk Exchange. Accelerant said it has no ownership stake in WoodStar but will provide underwriting, distribution, claims, and other services under contractual agreements. WoodStar is expected to begin writing meaningful direct business on the Accelerant Risk Exchange in 2027.

item.title: Carpe Data rebrands as Carpe
item.description: Carpe (Carpe Data) , a provider of next-generation data solutions for the insurance industry, has rebranded as Carpe, reflecting its focus on AI-powered insurance decisioning rather than data alone. Carpe said it is using AI to accelerate software development, improve its existing data products, and build new underwriting and claims tools, including reasoning engines that answer underwriting questions in plain language and claims workspaces that analyze files and surface relevant facts. “Carpe is no longer just a data company.

item.title: Wilton Re Completes DXC Cloud Conversion
item.description: (Image source: Wilton Re homepage.) Wilton Re (Norwalk, Conn.) has completed the conversion of more than 400,000 policies to DXC Technology (Ashburn, Va.)’s cloud-based insurance policy administration platform. Building an AI-Ready Foundation By consolidating onto DXC’s cloud-based platform, Wilton Re has established a foundation for future AI-enabled workflow capabilities without requiring an additional core-system transformation, according to DXC. The program migrated policies from prior DXC technologies onto DXC’s Wealth Management Accelerator, a cloud-based policy administration platform, according to a DXC statement.

item.title: US Appeals Court Revives Hundreds of Private Lawsuits Linking Tylenol to Autism
item.description: A federal appeals court on Monday revived more than 500 private lawsuits against Tylenol maker Kenvue over the painkiller’s alleged link to autism. Circuit Court of Appeals in Manhattan said a district court judge improperly excluded expert testimony from three doctors offered by parents and guardians who tied Tylenol use during pregnancy to autism and attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) in children. In a 64-page decision for a three-judge panel, Circuit Judge Guido Calabresi said the testimony from the three doctors, including the dean of Harvard University’s School of Public Health, reflected methodologies used by other scientists, and “constitute acceptable interpretations of scientific evidence where scientists may, and in fact do, disagree.” Calabresi stressed that the appeals court was not deciding whether using acetaminophen causes autism or ADHD, or whether elected officials should do more to protect public health.

item.title: What to Know About the Widespread Cyclospora Outbreak
item.description: The exact source of one of the nation’s largest outbreaks in years of cyclospora infections is still not known. And it can be hard to figure out what food sick people had in common because sometimes it’s a single ingredient that might be common in multiple recipes — like basil or cilantro. Luckily cyclospora infections are generally treatable with antibiotics and rarely cause serious complications.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Slows as Iran Strikes Raise Safety Risk
item.description: The number of tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz fell in the past day to the lowest level in two months, shipping data showed on Monday, as renewed strikes between the U.S. “With global inventories rapidly depleted in recent months, this is a recipe for much tighter supply, higher prices and significant downside risk for tanker markets.” The Sea Faith oil products tanker was among the few visible vessels sailing towards the entrance to the Strait of Hormuz near the Iranian side of the waterway, with a destination of Sohar, according to LSEG and MarineTraffic ship-tracking data on Monday. Commercial traffic through the Strait of Hormuz “continued at reduced levels,” the U.S.

item.title: Study Finds Long-Term Substance Use Among Workers Rising
item.description: Traditional urine testing methods typically captures drug use within a window of several hours or days, while hair analysis offers a long-term history of substance use patterns over extended periods, offering insights into ongoing drug use behaviors. Nearly one in five workers in the general U.S. “Employers play a critical role in addressing the root causes of substance misuse through supportive policies, benefits and recovery.” In addition to marijuana, analysis showed hair test positivity for other substances also increased.

item.title: RBC analysts expect pricing pressures and higher cat losses to weigh on US insurers in Q2’26
item.description: RBC Capital Markets, the investment banking and capital markets business of Royal Bank of Canada, expects the US property and casualty (P&C) insurance sector to remain competitive, with softer primary property pricing and potentially elevated catastrophe losses likely to shape earnings across both insurers and insurance brokers. Analysts also expects catastrophe losses to play a more prominent role in second-quarter financial results than they did a year earlier. According to RBC Capital Markets, the property market continues to face competitive pricing conditions, particularly during the second quarter, which represents the busiest period for US property insurance renewals and accounts for approximately 30% of annual premiums.

item.title: Jeff Smith appointed CEO of newly created Ryan Specialty Renewables
item.description: Ryan Specialty Underwriting Managers (RSUM), the underwriting management division of Ryan Specialty, has announced the appointment of Jeff Smith as CEO of the newly created Ryan Specialty Renewables practice, effective immediately. Jeff Smith commented: “Ryan Specialty Renewables represents the next stage in our evolution as a global renewable energy and power platform. “I’m excited to see Jeff unite the strengths of PERse North America, PERse International and Albus to deliver the best of our three brands to clients, brokers and capital providers globally, positioning Ryan Specialty Renewables as a leading global practice focused on growth, execution and operational excellence.”

item.title: IAG submits remedy offer to ACCC regarding RACI acquisition
item.description: Insurance Australia Group Limited (IAG) has submitted a package of proposed remedies to the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) in a bid to address the regulator’s competition concerns over its proposed acquisition of RAC Insurance Pty Limited (RACI) from the Royal Automobile Club of Western Australia. However, in December 2025, the ACCC opposed the acquisition , noting that RACI is the market leader in motor vehicle and home and contents insurance in Western Australia, competing strongly through its well-recognised RAC WA brand, competitive pricing, and high-quality service, including claims experiences. The total consideration for the transaction was A$1.35 billion, comprising A$400 million for IAG’s acquisition of 100% of RACI’s shares, equivalent to its expected net tangible asset value at completion, and an upfront payment of A$950 million for the exclusive distribution and brand licensing agreement.

item.title: Appeals Court Revives Private Lawsuits Linking Tylenol to Autism
item.description: A federal appeals court on July 13 revived more than 500 private lawsuits against Tylenol maker Kenvue over the painkiller’s alleged link to autism. Circuit Court of Appeals in Manhattan said a district court judge improperly excluded testimony from expert witnesses offered by parents and guardians who tied Tylenol use during pregnancy to autism and attention deficit hyperactivity disorder in children. The private lawsuits were dismissed in December 2024 by U.S.

item.title: Meta Expands Louisiana Data Center to 5 Gigawatts, Investment Crosses $50B
item.description: Meta META.O said on Monday its data center in Richland Parish, Louisiana, will expand to 5 gigawatts of compute capacity, with investment in the project increasing to more than $50 billion. The planned data center, known as Hyperion, was earlier projected to deliver more than 2 gigawatts of compute capacity to support training of large language models, the technology behind tools such as ChatGPT. (Reporting by Jaspreet Singh in Bengaluru; Editing by Leroy Leo and Devika Syamnath) Get the insurance industry's trusted newsletter

item.title: New York, Northeast Will See Temperatures Soar This Week
item.description: New York City and the Northeast will see temperatures surge this week as another wave of sultry weather shifts across the eastern US and southern Canada. “Tuesday and Wednesday will be the two hotter days and then things will cool down gradually through the end of the week.” High temperatures can push up energy demand as people crank up air conditioners to keep cool. Accounting for the humidity, temperatures this week may feel as hot as 104F across Manhattan, the Bronx, Brooklyn, Queens and Staten Island, the National Weather Service said.

item.title: Plymouth Rock strengthens leadership team with three executive appointments
item.description: Plymouth Rock has appointed three senior executives to lead its data, claims and customer service functions, reinforcing its focus on analytics, artificial intelligence (AI) and operational transformation across the business. Claims leadership focused on AI adoption Cornelius Young  has been appointed  Vice President and Chief Claims Officer  for Plymouth Rock’s Independent Agency Group. AI remains a strategic priority The appointments underscore Plymouth Rock’s continued investment in data-driven decision-making and artificial intelligence across core insurance functions.

item.title: Zurich launches new global rating engine on Solveva’s Actus platform
item.description: Zurich Insurance has deployed a new Global Rating Engine built on Solveva’s Actus platform , marking the first milestone in a broader programme to modernise its commercial insurance pricing infrastructure. Beyond the rating engine itself, Zurich has already implemented several supporting capabilities on the Actus platform, including: Scenario analysis Master data management Calculation administration Additional functionality is expected to be introduced as the rollout continues. Modernising commercial insurance pricing The Global Rating Engine is designed to help Zurich’s underwriters and actuaries develop, maintain and deploy pricing models more efficiently.

item.title: OIP Insurtech launches workflow diagnostic to guide AI investment in insurance
item.description: OIP Insurtech has introduced a Workflow Intelligence Diagnostic , a fixed-fee assessment designed to help insurers identify operational inefficiencies and prioritize technology and AI investments before launching transformation programmes. The Workflow Intelligence Diagnostic is intended to establish that operational baseline by analysing processes across the insurance value chain before technology investments are made. The Diagnostic converts operational friction into measurable business impact and gives leadership a prioritised roadmap before significant transformation investment is made.

item.title: Plymouth Rock adds three executives
item.description: Plymouth Rock appointed three executives to leadership roles spanning data, claims and customer service. Cornelius Young was named vice president and chief claims officer for Plymouth Rock’s Independent Agency Group. Gavin McPhail joins as vice president and chief data officer, responsible for the insurer’s enterprise data strategy and its use of analytics, automation and AI across underwriting, claims, risk management and customer operations.

item.title: Southern Cross Travel Insurance launches real-time flight delay benefit
item.description: Southern Cross Travel Insurance partnered with Blink Parametric to launch TravelCare: Delay Assist, an automated flight delay benefit for customers in New Zealand and Australia. This new offering reflects our focus on making travel insurance simpler, more useful, and responsive to real customer needs.” – Carole Tokody, CEO at Southern Cross Travel Insurance. The benefit is initially included with International Comprehensive Single Trip policies purchased from July 1.

item.title: Rockstead rebrands as Broadstone Lending Advisory Services
item.description: Rockstead will change its name to Broadstone Lending Advisory Services following its acquisition by Broadstone in March 2026. The rebrand brings Rockstead’s lender advisory capabilities together with Broadstone’s broader services across governance, operational resilience, regulatory compliance and due diligence. The business will operate within Broadstone’s Banking & Credit Advisory division and continue providing risk management and operational services to lenders, funders and investors.

item.title: ISI Appoints Doug Caccese as CEO to Lead Next Phase of Growth
item.description: I’m confident he will be a tremendous addition to ISI and guide the company through its next phase of growth.” “ISI has built a reputation for being a true partner to its customers, and that reputation is what drew me here,” said Doug Caccese, Chief Executive Officer of ISI. For customers, it remains business as usual: they will continue working with the same trusted ISI team, while benefiting from additional executive experience to support the company’s next phase of growth and innovation. Doug Caccese has been appointed Chief Executive Officer of ISI.

item.title: NY AG Suing 3M, Other Chemical Companies Over PFAS Pollution
item.description: I look forward to ensuring the companies responsible for PFAS pollution are held accountable.” James alleges that the companies violated New York laws by producing, marketing and selling products containing chemicals they knew to be harmful to New Yorkers’ health and the environment for decades. New York Attorney General Letitia James on Thursday sued some of the nation’s largest chemical and agricultural companies — 3M Company, EIDP Inc., The Chemours Company Inc., Corteva Inc., and DuPont De Nemours Inc. — for contributing to decades of toxic polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) pollution in the state through their use in consumer products.

item.title: Soft P/C Market May Be Shallower Than Previous Cycles: Swiss Re
item.description: But Swiss Re said the current underwriting cycle may prove shallower than past soft markets as rising repair costs and growing catastrophe exposures limit pricing corrections. Swiss Re said the current cycle may be shallower than past soft markets, with insurers likely to reprice more sharply if large losses, inflation and capital signals deteriorate beyond expectations. Personal lines prices have started softening across major markets, Swiss Re said, noting that growth is shifting from rate-driven to volume-driven as insurers compete on price and terms rather than riding rate increases.

item.title: OneDegree promotes Emily Chow to chief executive
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Dhipaya braces for heavy claims after deadly Bangkok bar fire
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Guy Carpenter’s Cait Fisher embarks on new chapter in Bermuda
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Welcome To The ‘Show Me’ Era: Sapphire Ventures’ Anders Ranum On What Separates Winning AI Startups From The Rest
item.description: To understand how institutional capital is navigating this gap, Crunchbase News recently interviewed Anders Ranum , a partner at Sapphire Ventures . Meanwhile, private market valuations for AI startups continue to hit record highs. Ranum has spent nearly 15 years at the firm, where he focuses on B2B enterprise software, security and industrial infrastructure.

item.title: Cat loss retro rates fall up to 20% at mid-year renewals for loss-free accounts: Gallagher Re
item.description: Market conditions remained generally favourable for buyers of non-marine retrocession at the July renewals, according to reinsurance broker Gallagher Re, who highlights in its latest 1st View report, that catastrophe rates fell by up to 20% for loss free accounts. For non-marine retrocession, risk loss-free rates renewed -5% to -10% at July 1st, while catastrophe loss-free rates decreased by -10% to -20%. Backed by this healthy supply, buyers showed increased interest in expanding their aggregate and frequency protection at the mid-year renewals, as supply sufficiently met demand.

item.title: Ledger Investing reports continued demand for casualty ILS amid softening property cat market
item.description: Against the softening property catastrophe market, casualty remains the most focal alternative within the insurance-linked securities (ILS) space, but also the hardest to successfully close, according to Ledger Investing, who reports that while capital is actively seeking diversification, casualty rate adequacy remains intact, and the market is working deliberately to standardise. The insurtech and casualty ILS specialist detailed these dynamics in its recently published mid-year 2026 report, highlighting how the softening property market is creating further opportunities within the expanding casualty ILS sector. “Against a softening property-cat market, casualty remains the most-discussed alternative in ILS and, at the same time, the hardest to successfully close.

item.title: MSIG USA expands Board of Directors with three senior industry appointments
item.description: MSIG USA, the US specialty insurance arm of the global MS&AD Insurance Group, has expanded the Board of Directors of MSIG Holdings (USA), Inc. The three appointments bring a broad range of experience across finance, insurance regulation, technology and corporate strategy. Stafford joins the Board following a career spanning more than three decades in financial leadership, capital markets, governance and enterprise risk management.

item.title: AM Best maintains stable outlook on Guatemala’s insurance industry
item.description: Global credit rating agency AM Best is maintaining its stable outlook on Guatemala’s insurance industry, citing market expansion supported by economic growth and controlled inflation. “Guatemala’s insurance industry in 2025 maintained premium sufficiency, showing stability in claims and acquisition costs, demonstrating the market’s ability to navigate the different cycles in reinsurance,” said Inger Rodriguez, financial analyst, AM Best. According to the latest report, “Market Segment Outlook: Guatemala Insurance,” the country’s GDP growth is anticipated to moderate in 2026, though it will continue to be driven by private consumption and remittance inflows.

item.title: Gallagher Re appoints Michael Cipolla as VP, Facultative
item.description: Gallagher & Co., has announced the appointment of Michael Cipolla as Vice President, Facultative. Cipolla joins Gallagher Re from Everest, where he held several senior leadership roles, most recently serving as Casualty Chief Underwriting Officer of Everest Evolution, the dedicated U.S. Gallagher Re, the global reinsurance broking and advisory business of Arthur J.
